name: Asmodeus
description: Asmodeus, also Ashmedai in some traditions, is a prominent figure in Jewish folklore and demonology, later appearing in Christian and Islamic texts. His origins are debated, with some scholars suggesting a possible derivation from the Zoroastrian demon Aeshma Daeva. He is generally depicted as a powerful demon king, often associated with lust, destruction, and gambling. Asmodeus is described in the Book of Tobit as being responsible for the deaths of Sarah's seven husbands, preventing them from consummating their marriages. He is often portrayed as having multiple heads, including those of a bull, ram, or man, and possessing serpentine legs. His presence signifies chaos and the subversion of divine order, making him a formidable adversary in religious narratives. He is believed to hold sway over earthly pleasures and is a tempter of mortals, leading them astray with vices and temptations. His character embodies the darker aspects of human nature and the constant struggle between good and evil.
subtitle: King of Demons

keyMyths.title: Asmodeus and the Book of Tobit
keyMyths.description: In the Book of Tobit, Asmodeus is portrayed as a jealous demon who murders Sarah's seven successive husbands on their wedding nights, preventing the marriages from being consummated. The angel Raphael instructs Tobiah on how to overcome Asmodeus by burning the heart and liver of a fish, the smell of which drives the demon away. Raphael then pursues Asmodeus to Upper Egypt, where he binds him, allowing Tobiah and Sarah to finally marry. This myth demonstrates Asmodeus's destructive power and his ultimate defeat by divine intervention.

keyMyths.title: Asmodeus and King Solomon
keyMyths.description: Jewish legends recount Asmodeus's interactions with King Solomon during the construction of the First Temple. Solomon is said to have tricked Asmodeus into helping him build the temple by using a magical ring. During his captivity, Asmodeus cleverly outwitted Solomon, even managing to usurp the throne for a period, demonstrating his cunning and ability to deceive. Ultimately, Solomon regained his power, but the story highlights Asmodeus's intellectual prowess and his challenge to even the wisest of kings.

keyMyths.title: Asmodeus and the Well of Secrets
keyMyths.description: Some traditions depict Asmodeus as the guardian of hidden knowledge and secrets, particularly those related to magic and the underworld. He is believed to reside near or control access to a well or portal that leads to these secrets. Those seeking forbidden knowledge often try to invoke or bargain with Asmodeus, but at a great cost, as he is known for his deceitful nature and the heavy price he demands for his assistance. This highlights his association with forbidden knowledge and the dangers of seeking it through demonic means.
